subtasks.description: Query vendor reliability data including incident counts, SLA compliance, historical outages, and other risk indicators to calculate vendor risk scores
Classify each vendor into risk tiers (low_risk, medium_risk, high_risk) based on calculated risk scores and incident history thresholds

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) sql_gen (mid tier) to query vendor reliability metrics from the data warehouse—this requires multi-table joins across vendor, incident, and SLA tables to compute comprehensive risk scores; (2) cross_recon (mid tier) to apply classification logic based on the risk score output and incident history thresholds. Cross_recon is appropriate here because it reconciles quantitative scores with classification rules and generates the risk tier output. Both are mid-tier because they involve moderate complexity (multi-table aggregation and threshold-based decision logic) but not extreme volume, window functions, or multi-source reconciliation. We do NOT add a policy_action subtask because risk classification is a technical posture decision, not a governance/compliance policy evaluation."

output.sql: WITH vendor_metrics AS (SELECT vendor_name, COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'overdue' THEN 1 END) AS overdue_invoices, COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN 1 END) AS paid_invoices, COUNT(*) AS total_invoices, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS payment_compliance_rate, ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(DAY FROM paid_date - due_date)), 2) AS avg_days_late FROM invoice_facts WHERE invoice_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY vendor_name), risk_scores AS (SELECT vendor_name, total_invoices, overdue_invoices, paid_invoices, payment_compliance_rate, avg_days_late, ROUND((overdue_invoices::NUMERIC / NULLIF(total_invoices, 0) * 40) + (CASE WHEN COALESCE(avg_days_late, 0) > 30 THEN 30 ELSE (COALESCE(avg_days_late, 0) / 30) * 30 END) + (CASE WHEN payment_compliance_rate < 70 THEN 30 ELSE (payment_compliance_rate / 100) * 30 END), 2) AS risk_score FROM vendor_metrics) SELECT vendor_name, total_invoices, overdue_invoices, paid_invoices, payment_compliance_rate, avg_days_late, risk_score, CASE WHEN risk_score < 25 THEN 'low_risk' WHEN risk_score >= 25 AND risk_score < 60 THEN 'medium_risk' ELSE 'high_risk' END AS risk_classification FROM risk_scores ORDER BY risk_score DESC, vendor_name
